title: UNIST Develops Scalable Artificial Leaf for Green Hydrogen Production
content: In the race toward carbon-neutral energy, a major milestone has emerged from South Korea. Researchers at UNIST (Ulsan National Institute of Science and Technology) have developed a next-generation modular artificial leaf that generates hydrogen directly from sunlight and waterâ€”without external electricity or carbon emissions. The technology, inspired by natural photosynthesis, is designed to produce green hydrogen, a clean fuel hailed as a future alternative to fossil energy. Unlike conventional solar panel-electrolysis systems, the artificial leaf skips the electrical conversion step altogether, converting sunlight directly into chemical energy.
author: Analysts Account
